author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
verses:
gāyata rādhā mādhava⋅līlaṁ,
kuruta matiṁ rasarañjita⋅śīlām

vṛndāraṇya carācara vṛndaṁ
śrayata mahā⋅rasa vaibhava⋅vandam

paśyata rādhā keli nikuñjaṁ
prakaṭa mahādbhuta⋅rati⋅rasa⋅puñjam

puline puline tapana⋅sutāyāḥ
bhramatha na jatra prasarati⋅māyā

paramānanda rasāmṛta⋅sāre
nayata mano vraja⋅rāja⋅kumāre

muñcata viṣaya⋅viṣama rasa⋅gandhaṁ,
ghaṭayata haripade dṛḍha⋅rati⋅bandham

mṛgayata rādhā⋅pada⋅rasa⋅bhājaṁ,
paricinūtojvalara⋅rasa bāram

iti hita⋅sāra sarasvatī⋅gītaṁ,
janayata kañcana bhāvam⋅adhitama
en_translation:
Sing of the pastimes of Rādhā and Mādhava. Make your mind joyful with pure transcendental mellows.

In the Vṛndāvana forest, the multitude of all living beings takes shelter in the adoration of its great opulent mellows.

Behold Rādhā's playful grove, where an abundance of wonderful divine love is manifested.

On every sandy bank of the Yamunā, wander where illusion does not spread.

Bring your mind to the Son of Nanda Mahārāja, who is the essence of the nectar of supreme bliss.

Give up the poisonous scent of material objects and fix a firm attachment at the feet of Lord Hari.

Seek those devoted to the mellow of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī's feet, realizing the brilliant essence of divine love.

Thus this song by Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī, ardently presented, generates some spiritual emotion.
